1.solution: Row n has 2n−1 terms; last number = n². Row 16: last=256, first=225+1=226, count=31. Sum=(226+256)/2×31=241×31=7471.

2.solution: Sₐ=b, S_b=a. From Sₐ and S_b: d=−2(a+b)/(ab). S_{a+b}=(a+b)/2×[2α+(a+b−1)d]. Substituting 2α+ad=2b/a: S_{a+b}=(a+b)/2×[2b/a+bd]=(a+b)/2×[2b/a+b×(−2(a+b)/(ab))]=(a+b)/2×(−2)=−(a+b). Answer: A.

3.solution: For two APs with Sₙ ratio=(6n+2):(11n+3), the ratio of kth terms = ratio of Sₙ at n=2k−1. For k=14: n=27. Ratio=(6×27+2)/(11×27+3)=164/300=41/75. Answer: B.

5.solution: a(1+r)=10/7 and ar²/(1−r)=9/28. From second: ar²=9(1-r)/28. Dividing by first: r²/(1+r)=9/(40). Cross-multiply with first eq: 280r²=63(1−r²) → 343r²=63 → r=3/7. a(1+3/7)=10/7 → a=1. 7×second term=7×ar=7×3/7=3.
